title: Duplicate layers in Image inspect information build using podman build #1744
body:
Pulled from https://github.com/containers/libpod/issues/1744
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST?**:

[//]: **kind bug**


**Description**
I found this issue with this [particular](https://github.com/couchbase/docker/tree/master/enterprise/couchbase-server/4.0.0) image build using `podman build`. It shows same layer multiple times when `podman inspect` is done on image.

It is easily reproducible. I verified with `docker build` and there is no issue with that.


<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. git clone https://github.com/couchbase/docker 

2. cd docker/enterprise/couchbase-server/4.0.0

3. sudo podman  build --no-cache -f Dockerfile -t layers .

4. sudo podman inspect localhost/layers

**Describe the results you received:**
```
.
. <snip>
.

        },

On Ubuntu, physical machine.

comments.body:
Dockerfile used:

```
FROM ubuntu:14.04

MAINTAINER Couchbase Docker Team <docker@couchbase.com>

# Install dependencies:
#  runit: for container process management
#  wget: for downloading .deb
#  python-httplib2: used by CLI tools
#  chrpath: for fixing curl, below
#  tzdata: timezone info used by some N1QL functions
# Additional dependencies for system commands used by cbcollect_info:
#  lsof: lsof
#  lshw: lshw
#  sysstat: iostat, sar, mpstat
#  net-tools: ifconfig, arp, netstat
#  numactl: numactl
RUN apt-get update && \
    apt-get install -yq runit wget python-httplib2 chrpath tzdata \
    lsof lshw sysstat net-tools numactl  && \
    apt-get autoremove && apt-get clean && \
    rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/* /tmp/* /var/tmp/*

ARG CB_VERSION=4.0.0
ARG CB_RELEASE_URL=https://packages.couchbase.com/releases
ARG CB_PACKAGE=couchbase-server-enterprise_4.0.0-ubuntu14.04_amd64.deb
ARG CB_SHA256=c4fad00fe6006a31a82aa4879e7ae502cb9d397339e6d28f352312a0a5be9edd

ENV PATH=$PATH:/opt/couchbase/bin:/opt/couchbase/bin/tools:/opt/couchbase/bin/install

# Create Couchbase user with UID 1000 (necessary to match default
# boot2docker UID)
RUN groupadd -g 1000 couchbase && useradd couchbase -u 1000 -g couchbase -M

# Install couchbase
RUN export INSTALL_DONT_START_SERVER=1 && \
    wget -N $CB_RELEASE_URL/$CB_VERSION/$CB_PACKAGE && \
    echo "$CB_SHA256  $CB_PACKAGE" | sha256sum -c - && \
    dpkg -i ./$CB_PACKAGE && rm -f ./$CB_PACKAGE

# Add runit script for couchbase-server
COPY scripts/run /etc/service/couchbase-server/run
RUN chown -R couchbase:couchbase /etc/service

# Add dummy script for commands invoked by cbcollect_info that
# make no sense in a Docker container
COPY scripts/dummy.sh /usr/local/bin/
RUN ln -s dummy.sh /usr/local/bin/iptables-save && \
    ln -s dummy.sh /usr/local/bin/lvdisplay && \
    ln -s dummy.sh /usr/local/bin/vgdisplay && \
    ln -s dummy.sh /usr/local/bin/pvdisplay

# Fix curl RPATH
RUN chrpath -r '$ORIGIN/../lib' /opt/couchbase/bin/curl

# Add bootstrap script
COPY scripts/entrypoint.sh /
ENTRYPOINT ["/entrypoint.sh"]
CMD ["couchbase-server"]

# 8091: Couchbase Web console, REST/HTTP interface
# 8092: Views, queries, XDCR
# 8093: Query services (4.0+)
# 8094: Full-text Search (4.5+)
# 8095: Analytics (5.5+)
# 8096: Eventing (5.5+)
# 11207: Smart client library data node access (SSL)
# 11210: Smart client library/moxi data node access
# 11211: Legacy non-smart client library data node access
# 18091: Couchbase Web console, REST/HTTP interface (SSL)
# 18092: Views, query, XDCR (SSL)
# 18093: Query services (SSL) (4.0+)
# 18094: Full-text Search (SSL) (4.5+)
# 18095: Analytics (SSL) (5.5+)
# 18096: Eventing (SSL) (5.5+)
EXPOSE 8091 8092 8093 8094 8095 8096 11207 11210 11211 18091 18092 18093 18094 18095 18096
VOLUME /opt/couchbase/var
```
@kunalkushwaha just as a point of reference, do the containers built from this image work OK?  As noted in the Podman issue, this appears to be an issue somewhere in Buildah, digging further.
Yes, the image works perfectly fine so far.
Is the tree implementation supposed ot be a fix for this?
@rhatdan No, this is build time information written in image metadata. Needs to be fixed  in buildah.
@kunalkushwaha Do you know if this still exists?  @nalind @TomSweeneyRedHat

I think this one may have been fixed with @nalind's latest round of fixes.  The layers are much smaller and non-repetitive when built with Buildah, but I can't easily test using Podman as Buildah hasn't been vendored there yet.  @nalind or @kunalkushwaha WDYT?

I verified this bug again with same steps as mentioned above.

Image generated by latest `buildah` have less layers as it is with `docker`, but also it do not have duplicate layers as earlier. So there is no bug, but output is still different.

It seems `buildah` is not adding layers with each `RUN` , `COPY` command and or merging them with previous layer one successful. Please see the description below.

_Output by buildah  `version 1.8-dev (image-spec 1.0.0, runtime-spec 1.0.0)`_
```
        "RootFS": {                
            "Type": "layers",                                                                         
            "Layers": [                                          
                "sha256:eef560b4ec4f5e97b601fb527f7f2e61593b4a4882a1de4a7a6cd3bd5a73f7ea",                                                                                                     
                "sha256:11a0c2f551fda1783ba1c36dc0ef47dff32d9b1f6d1f1e4c09c6c59b282663a8", 
                "sha256:dda5ec330bd9a52aa8e2d73a25c2c4986cb528538e7fdc95adcdb2a65485e3d6",

                "sha256:03be3c4f2a96e5bb0218f026d858d3a861156e1efa3117e77c2c16fdf4062ea3"
            ]                                                    
        },
```

_layers generated by `docker build`_
```
       "RootFS": {
            "Type": "layers",
            "Layers": [
                "sha256:eef560b4ec4f5e97b601fb527f7f2e61593b4a4882a1de4a7a6cd3bd5a73f7ea",

                "sha256:2f465e7e01e66c1bc8d5a3bcf88bfc1e2453026e154d0293efa8e754e325f475",

                "sha256:c039f0eaf7fab64bd0a982e8e46d2ca71c814f0a494c000a9352ddffce295ae2",
                "sha256:fe1e413501826e990bef5adcecebb718cffa533c471da626e06fdef3b806ab34",
                "sha256:acb9aa2d9652cca4024d15741f174faef5b2968aa6a380620167f617b5baefe1",

Also observed the original image `docker.io/library/ubuntu:14.04` have only 4 layers. This is verified in both `podman` & `docker`
```
$ sudo  podman image tree docker.io/library/ubuntu:14.04
Image ID: 390582d83ead
Tags:    [docker.io/library/ubuntu:14.04]
Size:    197.2MB
Image Layers
├──  ID: eef560b4ec4f Size:   197MB
├──  ID: f488197f2fc3 Size: 209.9kB
├──  ID: 82cc10ad4644 Size: 7.168kB
└──  ID: d7321d669147 Size: 3.072kB Top Layer of: [docker.io/library/ubuntu:14.04]
```
Buildah adds one layer on top of it. 
```
$ sudo podman image tree  localhost/layers              
Image ID: fe6a51691286
Tags:    [localhost/layers:latest]
Size:    444.4MB
Image Layers
├──  ID: eef560b4ec4f Size:   197MB
├──  ID: f488197f2fc3 Size: 209.9kB
├──  ID: 82cc10ad4644 Size: 7.168kB
├──  ID: d7321d669147 Size: 3.072kB Top Layer of: [docker.io/library/ubuntu:14.04]
└──  ID: b53264bf5cd9 Size: 247.2MB Top Layer of: [localhost/layers:latest]
```

whereas docker adds 9 layers to base four layers. one layer for each `RUN` & `COPY` command in dockerfile.

```
        "RootFS": {
            "Type": "layers",
            "Layers": [
                "sha256:eef560b4ec4f5e97b601fb527f7f2e61593b4a4882a1de4a7a6cd3bd5a73f7ea",
                "sha256:11a0c2f551fda1783ba1c36dc0ef47dff32d9b1f6d1f1e4c09c6c59b282663a8",
                "sha256:dda5ec330bd9a52aa8e2d73a25c2c4986cb528538e7fdc95adcdb2a65485e3d6",
                "sha256:5f96fa66dc12213040af6a47414ed4652c05ac1b9573ceb55b46bb83cf616892",
                "sha256:16bd7779bfac143d231938c3a58423a8835d30b451a72db6698d11b78deb163e",
                "sha256:5bb5f6f6689dfda1c6527c9cc323ebaee98ab842ebae79fc02f9fdd937e808da",
                "sha256:d3977d512ee1ee316ae29e64de95cc1c7efe7b601e0acbafb42c9be54308e9dc",
                "sha256:2f465e7e01e66c1bc8d5a3bcf88bfc1e2453026e154d0293efa8e754e325f475",
                "sha256:df4d48fb5cef1aba81a6c88220523f6926e0f08e68f94747fba12dc0ebe24004",
                "sha256:c039f0eaf7fab64bd0a982e8e46d2ca71c814f0a494c000a9352ddffce295ae2",
                "sha256:fe1e413501826e990bef5adcecebb718cffa533c471da626e06fdef3b806ab34",
                "sha256:acb9aa2d9652cca4024d15741f174faef5b2968aa6a380620167f617b5baefe1",
                "sha256:42c366f43899a87cd68c7ebef6eeb2ca91203308b9b539991d5ce45e96ab0c88"
            ]

```

> Did you run buildah bud --layers?

No. I was not aware of this option. Will update on this tomorrow.

Kunal Kushwaha

Image generated with `bud --layers` options, generate all layers.

Though one small difference from `docker build`. buildah generates one extra layer. 
i.e. Docker generates 9 layers, whereas buildah generates 10 layers. (No layers are duplicate) 

```
$ sudo podman image tree localhost/layers                                                                                                                                                                                                                       
Image ID: 24a458904115                                                                                                                                                                         
Tags:    [localhost/layers:latest]                                                                                                                                                             
Size:    445.7MB                                                                                                                                                                               
Image Layers

```
I think original bug is fixed,
@nalind is working on fixing this issue. Basically buildah creates a final image even if the last command in the Dockerfile is not COPY, ADD, or RUN, whereas Docker does not.
